revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=神明神社 | 画像=[[File:Shinmei Shrine(Fukui city).jpg|300px|神明神社 (福井市)]] | 所在地=福井県福井市宝永4-8-1 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=天照皇大神 | 社格=旧県社・別表神社 | 創建=延長2年（924年） | 本殿=[[神明造]][[鋼板葺]] | 例祭=[[5月5日]]（神明祭） }}
'''神明神社'''（しんめいじんじゃ）は、[[福井県]][[福井市]]宝永にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。'''お神明さん'''（おしんめさん）の通称で呼ばれる。[[天照大神]]を祀る。
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[醍醐天皇]]の時代、内宮足羽御厨（みくりや）の北の庄の某が伊勢皇大神の勧請を願い出て勅許され、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]2年（[[924年]]）に神霊を[[足羽神社]]に祀り、間もなく現在地に社殿を造営して遷座したという。
『[[吾妻鏡]]』には、[[建久]]3年（[[1192年]]）、[[源頼朝]]が妹の一条能保の室に当社の領家職を与えたとの記述がある。歴代領主の崇敬を受け、[[江戸幕府]]は[[朱印領]]百石を寄進した。明治4年に県社に列格した。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 神明神社]（福井県神社庁）

revision.comment: Created page with "{{神社 | 名称=Shinmei Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | 所在地=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | 位置={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | 社格=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | 創建=Enchō 2 (924) | 本殿={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}..."
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=Shinmei Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | 所在地=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | 位置={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | 社格=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | 創建=Enchō 2 (924) | 本殿={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | 例祭=May 5 (Shinmei Festival) | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.

It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui City]] {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社[[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]], Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}}, {{ill|Fukui, Fukui|ja|福井市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. The shrine’s former rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].

==History==
According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|qq=|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.

The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|qq=|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.

 | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine

 | honden=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]]

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].

==History==
According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.

The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.

==External Links==
*[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.

The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].

==History==
According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.

The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]][[ja:神明神社 (福井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590317]] Wikidata name: Shinmei Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location=[[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui]] [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui City]] [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]], [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]].
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The [[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]] records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Shinmei Shrine | image=[[File:Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city).jpg|300px|Shinmei Shrine (Fukui city)]] | location={{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui|1=Fukui Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui City|1=Fukui (city)}} {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}} 4-8-1 | coordinates={{Coord|36|04|07|N|136|13|10|E|scale:25000_region:JP-18|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} | shakaku=Former Prefectural Shrine・Beppyo Shrine | established=Enchō 2 (924) | honden={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} [[Steel plate roofing|鋼板葺]] | main_festival=May 5 (Shinmei festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukui Prefecture|label=Shinmei Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Shinmei Shrine''' (神明神社, Shinmei Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|WD=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}}, {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui, Fukui|1=Fukui (city)}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. The shrine’s former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is commonly referred to as "Oshinmesan" (おしんめさん) and enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}.
==History== According to the shrine's tradition, during the era of Emperor Daigo, a certain individual from Hokuno-no-shō of the Naikū Ashihara Mikuriya requested and received imperial permission to enshrine the deity of the Grand Shrine of Ise. In Enchō 2 (924), the divine spirit was enshrined at {{ill|Asuwa Shrine|ja|足羽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuwa Shrine|1=Asuwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637049}}, and soon after, a shrine building was constructed at the current location for the deity.
The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}} records that in Kenkyū 3 (1192), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} granted the position of lord of the shrine to his sister's husband, {{ill|Ichijō Yoshiyasu|ja|一条能保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō Yoshiyasu|1=Ichijō Yoshiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352829}}. The shrine enjoyed the patronage of successive lords, and the Edo Shogunate granted it a stipend of 100 koku. In Meiji 4, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine.
==External Links== *[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160119_155650 Shinmei Shrine] (Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office)
